# Transcrição: Breaking_the_Illusion_of_Confidence

- **Fonte:** Breaking_the_Illusion_of_Confidence
- **Duração:** 02:10
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 02:10]

To be more confident is just a series of behaviors. So right now, you most likely perceive me as being confident. So me being confident is a series of behaviors. It's me using my body language in a certain way, facial expressions, my voice, the volume at which I'm speaking at. All of this is hopefully radiating someone who's confident. Whereas if I change some behaviors, and let's say, for example, let's say I do this now and I'm not giving you eye contact. I'm speaking with a lower volume, playing smaller with my body language. All of a sudden, you perceive me to be shy. So I always like to demystify it for my students in that shy and confident are a different set of behaviors. So you've practiced and you're very familiar with shy behaviors. What you have to do is you have to just practice the more confident behaviors. And at the start, and a lot of people don't like this, they don't like the whole Oh, fake it till you make it is bad. Whether it's fake it till you make it or try to be the best version of yourself or practice the confident version of yourself, use whatever metaphor you want. But essentially what I'm saying is fake it till you make it. Pretend to be confident. I remember the first time that I practiced being confident. You know, I used to be really shy, right? I used to be really good at all the shy behaviors because I've been practicing them and anchoring them my whole life. So when I started to practice being more confident, the first thing I practiced was I practice speaking with more volume. So I just focus on one key behavioral change at a time. Again, the body language hasn't changed. I didn't really give people eye contact yet, but I said, Hi, it's really great to meet you. And I just changed one behavior at a time. All I would just share with you is that practice the confident behaviors and practice them one at a time. First thing being volume, second thing being eye contact. And third thing being used larger body language and just start practicing it. And this is what really happens. It's fascinating. And then as you pretend to do this, even though you feel shy on the inside, then people around you will start to say, Hey, you seem really confident. And the first time they say that, you go, Oh, yeah, yeah, yeah, yeah, yeah, yeah. Yeah, yeah, I am confident. Yeah. And then you practice stronger voice, bigger body language, better eye contact. And then soon people say, Hey, you seem really confident. And you go, Yeah, yeah, I am confident. Damn it. It's always this funny process of learning a new set of behaviors that help you come across in a certain way. And then when people perceive you to be that way, you start to also perceive yourself to be that way.
